scope: Typed resources reflection for source-backed concept-film preparation, visual audit, caption sidecar, loss-safe compression, and the unselected narration boundary.

evidence.subject: Credential-free Vox workspace preparation validates source provenance and OCR-clean generated posters.
evidence.observation: The focused preparation suite passed 4 of 4 tests, including source-path policy, output preservation, and a no OCR-detectable text poster check.

evidence.subject: Stage-six clean-plate poster audit confirms visual repair quality.
evidence.observation: The current kf_6a and kf_6b posters have no readable baked UI/text or repair silhouettes; both retain coherent painterly torn-paper composition and a readable command/legion subject.

evidence.subject: Silent visual concept-film output passes the local web-video and quality-compression gates.
evidence.observation: The 36-second output is H.264/yuv420p, 960x540 at 24fps, faststart, and fully decodable. CRF24/slow compression produced SSIM 0.997062 and reduced bytes by 5.421% from the retained baseline.

evidence.subject: The canonical concept-film contract keeps narration and audio provider selection explicit.
evidence.observation: The six Korean narration lines remain within the stated length budget, but voice_provider is unselected and the contract requires selected provider/model-or-voice, checksum, loudness review, human approval, and Playwriter review before a voiced film can enter runtime packaging.

findings.statement: No narration/audio provider is selected, so the silent visual artifact and sidecar captions are not eligible to replace the current locally rendered runtime fallback.

corrective_actions.action: Select a Korean narration/audio provider, generate or import the source with its model-or-voice record, checksum and loudness review, then obtain human and Playwriter approval before changing runtime media.
corrective_actions.acceptance: A selected and documented Korean audio source has checksum and loudness evidence, a matching caption and descriptive transcript, and explicit human and Playwriter approval for runtime packaging.

next_stage_decision.rationale: Visual source preparation and loss-safe local encoding are evidenced, but the required narration source and approvals are absent; retain the current runtime fallback.
